book_summary: Exodus has chapters 1-12 complete in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The completed material preserves names against erasure, Israel's creation-blessing multiplication, Pharaoh's fear-based oppression, Shiphrah and Puah as named life-protectors, Moses' water-rescue through women's agency, Divine hearing/remembering/seeing/knowing, burning bush, YHWH name disclosure as I AM / I WILL BE, liberation commission, speech accommodation through Aaron, Pharaoh confrontation, intensified brick labor, covenant reassurance, signs against Pharaoh's hardened system, Goshen distinction, favor among Egyptians, Pesach/Passover, unleavened bread, mixed multitude, one instruction for native and sojourner, and liberation night.

chapters.notes: Names, multiplication, forced labor, midwives' resistance, Pharaoh's death-command.
Moses birth/rescue, women's agency, Moses' complicated intervention, Midian, Divine hearing covenant groaning.
Burning bush, holy ground, YHWH name disclosure, Divine seeing/hearing/knowing, liberation commission.
Signs, Moses' speech objection, Aaron as support, return to Egypt, firstborn confrontation, Zipporah episode, elders believe.
First Pharaoh confrontation, worsened brick labor, Israelite foremen, Moses' lament.
YHWH covenant reassurance, liberation verbs, crushed breath/spirit, genealogy of Moses/Aaron.
Moses/Aaron roles, staff-serpent sign, Nile blood/life sign, magicians, hardening.
Frogs, gnats/lice, swarm, magicians' limit, Goshen distinction, partial concessions.
Livestock pestilence, boils, hail, Egyptians who heed warning, Pharaoh confession without turning.
Locusts, darkness, Pharaoh's servants protest, partial liberation bargaining, final rupture.
Final plague announcement, favor in Egypt, firstborn judgment framed against Pharaoh's death policy.
Pesach/Passover, unleavened bread, firstborn judgment, mixed multitude, one Torah for native and sojourner, liberation night.

translation_notes: Hebrew-first approach required: Shemot/Names, YHWH, Elohim, avad/avodah, berit, ruach, pesach, mishkan, kavod, chesed where relevant.
Avad/avodah is a central thread: Pharaoh turns service into crushing bondage; YHWH liberates for covenant service/worship.
YHWH name disclosure in Exodus 3:14 preserves Hebrew verbal ambiguity: I AM / I WILL BE.
Pharaoh hardening language must track Hebrew verbs carefully: hazaq, kaved, qashah require later lexical audit.
Do not demonize Egyptians ethnically; critique Pharaoh, empire, forced labor, and fear-based domination.
Women's agency is central in Exodus 1-2 and should remain visible.
Zipporah's circumcision episode is obscure; preserve ambiguity and avoid making it a ritual template.
Speech/disability material must not shame Moses; the narrative gives presence, teaching, and Aaron as accommodation/partnership.
Land promise and named peoples require later ethical audit, especially before conquest material.
Pesach/Passover should be preserved as Hebrew liberation memorial, not flattened into later theology before Exodus is heard on its own terms.
The firstborn judgment must remain tethered to Pharaoh's firstborn death policy and repeated hardened refusal.
Mixed multitude in Exodus 12:38 is crucial for liberation's multi-people dimension.
One Torah/instruction for native and sojourner in Exodus 12:49 should be preserved as covenant inclusion with boundary markers.
Pharaoh's partial concessions are control tactics and should be tracked as anti-liberation bargaining.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Exodus 3:14 YHWH/Ehyeh asher ehyeh requires deep lexical and reception-history audit.
Exodus 4:21 and hardening motif should receive verb-by-verb audit across Exodus.
Exodus 4:24-26 Zipporah circumcision episode requires source, syntax, and reception-history note.
Exodus land promise / named peoples requires ethical and historical reception audit before later conquest texts.
Exodus 7-12 sign/plague sequence requires ecological, literary, and anti-ethnic-flattening audit.
Exodus 12 Pesach/Passover terminology and calendar reset require Hebrew ritual/source audit.
Exodus 12:38 mixed multitude and 12:49 one Torah for native/sojourner require covenant inclusion audit.
next_actions: Continue Exodus with chapters 13-15: consecration of firstborn, unleavened bread memorial, pillar/cloud/fire, sea crossing, and Song of the Sea.
